title: View Mode Character Mnemonics
text: View Mode Character Mnemonics These mnemonics define the characters used in View mode (for example, on the paint canvas or in a field). There are no defaults for the View mode characters. If the mnemonics are not defined, the appropriate characters from Font 7 are used. Arguments Table 1. Mnemonics for View Mode Characters Mnemonic Explanation view_bot Entity bottom line. view_cr Carriage return character. view_fF Form feed character. view_frame Frame character. view_ht Horizontal tab character. view_mid Entity middle line. view_top Entity top line. view_vert Entity left or right vertical line. Description These characters are usually available in a device character set. If they are defined as part of a particular character set, Uniface will send the control codes to shift into this character set (if it is not already in this set), before displaying or printing the character. For example, these mnemonics are defined as part of character set 2 for a VT display. The definition for this set in the VT translation table is shown below: BEGIN_SET 2 special GRAPHIC set ^esc(0 select special graphic set selection ... b view_ht map HT (horizontal tab) c view_ff map FF (formfeed) d view_cr map CR (carret) o view_top map view entity top line o view_mid map view entity mid line s view_bot map view entity bottom line ~ view_vert map view entity left/right vertical END_SET Copy To output a carriage return character (identified by the view_cr mnemonic), Uniface first sends the codes associated with the select mnemonic for character set 2, then the character code associated with the view_cr mnemonic. Therefore, for the VT device, the following codes are sent to the device: ^027(0d Copy Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
